items.title: GPU demand continues to outpace supply as AI training scales up
items.content_text: Global GPU demand is projected to grow 45% year-over-year in 2026, driven by large language model training and inference workloads. Key suppliers face capacity constraints through Q3 2026.

items.title: Foundation model startups raise $12B in Q1 2026, up 30% from Q4 2025
items.content_text: Venture capital investment in foundation model companies reached $12 billion in Q1 2026, a 30% increase from the previous quarter. Investors are focusing on companies with proprietary data moats and vertical specialization.

items.title: Enterprise AI adoption accelerates with 67% of Fortune 500 deploying AI agents
items.content_text: A new survey shows 67% of Fortune 500 companies have deployed AI agents in production, up from 42% a year ago. Customer service, code generation, and data analysis are the top use cases.

items.title: Open-source models close gap with proprietary models on key benchmarks
items.content_text: Recent benchmarks show open-source language models have narrowed the performance gap with proprietary models to under 5% on reasoning tasks. This is disrupting pricing models and accelerating enterprise adoption of open-source alternatives.

items.title: EU AI Act compliance costs estimated at 2-5% of revenue for AI companies
items.content_text: Industry analysis estimates that compliance with the EU AI Act will cost AI companies between 2-5% of annual revenue. Companies with robust safety and governance frameworks are expected to gain competitive advantages.
